summary: Kenneth demonstrates his weekly covered call strategy on Hims, selling 1-week-out calls $1 above his $56 purchase price ($57 strike) to generate ~$180-205 per week in premium. He discusses the trade-off between higher strike prices (58-59) that risk early assignment versus rolling contracts to retain shares when stock moves significantly.
takeaways: Sell covered calls 1 week out ($1 above purchase price for predictable entry)
Target ~$180-205 premium per covered call on 100-share positions
Monitor for early assignment risk when underlying moves significantly ITM before Friday expiration
Consider rolling contracts to following week to preserve shares rather than repurchasing
Track weekly premium accumulation systematically in a playlist series
Current position: 100 shares Hims bought at $56, high probability of $58 strike assignment by June 20th expiration
Monitor geopolitical/macro events (Israel-Iran tensions mentioned) that can cause rapid directional moves mid-week

strategy_candidates.change: Establish predetermined rolling rules: automatically roll when stock moves >$1 ITM by Wednesday to preserve shares and extend premium collection
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth explicitly mentions rolling as an option to keep shares rather than lose them to assignment, but indicates he decides case-by-case; a preset rule would optimize the weekly premium desk consistency

title: PART 3: Can I make $180 per week selling covered calls?
